{"id":"broadside-205m","title":"DID identity layer (phase 1: emit, peers ignore)","description":"Add decentralized identifier support to actors. Phase 1 emits DID data that non-DID-aware peers ignore. Lays groundwork for DID-keyed relay/cache benefits in phase 2.

## Goals
- Every actor gets a did:key (self-certifying, survives server death)
- Every actor gets a did:web (resolvable, current home)
- Actor JSON includes DID fields (ignored by peers)
- DID documents served at standard paths

## Non-goals (phase 1)
- DID-based signature keyId (breaks compat)
- DID resolution on inbound (unnecessary yet)
- Follower tracking by DID (schema change, later)

## Success criteria
- Actor JSON has did field and alsoKnownAs with both DIDs
- GET /users/{username}/did.json returns valid DID document
- Federation with non-DID servers unchanged","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"mark@reviewcommit.com","created_at":"2026-07-19T03:54:44.116531228Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-19T03:54:44.116531228Z"}
new_value:
{"description":"Add decentralized identifier support to actors. Phase 1 emits DID data that non-DID-aware peers ignore. Lays groundwork for DID-keyed relay/cache benefits in phase 2.

## Goals
- Every actor gets a did:key (self-certifying, survives server death)
- Every actor gets a did:web (resolvable, current home)
- Every actor gets a did:plc (Bluesky-compatible, registry-backed)
- Users get recovery phrase (24 words, identity backup)
- Actor JSON includes DID fields (ignored by peers)
- DID documents served at standard paths

## Non-goals (phase 1)
- DID-based signature keyId (breaks compat)
- DID resolution on inbound (unnecessary yet)
- Follower tracking by DID (schema change, later)

## Success criteria
- Actor JSON has did field and alsoKnownAs with all three DIDs
- GET /users/{username}/did.json returns valid DID document
- did:plc registered with plc.directory (server + recovery rotation keys)
- User shown recovery phrase on signup
- Federation with non-DID servers unchanged"}

## Goals
- Every actor gets a did:key (self-certifying, survives server death)

{"design":"## Compatibility with gaja and smallhold

Must match these implementations exactly:

**did:key encoding**: Ed25519 multicodec (0xed01 prefix) + base58btc multibase (z prefix). Result: did:key:z6Mk... — gaja src/did.rs:15-25, smallhold src/did.rs:13-25.

**did:web format**: did:web:{domain}:users:{username} (dots→colons in domain). Computed, not stored. — gaja src/did.rs:29-37, smallhold src/did.rs:27-31.

**DID document** (W3C DID v1): Two verification methods — RsaVerificationKey2018 #main-key (AP signing key) + Ed25519VerificationKey2020 #recovery-key (publicKeyMultibase). Context includes https://www.w3.org/ns/did/v1 and https://w3id.org/security/suites/ed2020/v1. — gaja src/did.rs:69-103, smallhold src/did.rs:62-120.

**BIP-39 recovery**: 24-word mnemonic from 256-bit entropy. Private key never stored. Recovery pubkey hex-encoded in DB. — gaja src/did.rs:40-60, smallhold src/did.rs:33-54.

**Actor alsoKnownAs**: Array with did:web and did:key (and profile URL per smallhold). — gaja src/activitypub.rs:200-210, smallhold src/activitypub.rs:139-146.

**Endpoint**: GET /users/{username}/did.json, Content-Type: application/did+json — both projects.

**Dependencies**: ed25519-dalek v2 (features=[rand_core]), bip39 v2, multibase v0.9, zeroize v1.

**No did:plc**: AT Protocol specific (gaja only). Broadside does not need plc.directory registration.

**Recovery is per-persona**: Broadside has no user accounts. Each persona gets its own Ed25519 recovery keypair."}

All 11 tasks complete. DID identity layer fully implemented: did:key + did:web generation, BIP-39 recovery, DID document endpoint, actor alsoKnownAs, CLI backfill/recover, 5 federation verification tests. Compatible with gaja and smallhold.
